question_text: Which of the following statements correctly describes a parallel circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: Components are connected end-to-end in a single loop

options.label: B
options.text: The total current is the sum of currents through each branch

options.label: C
options.text: All components receive the same current

options.label: D
options.text: If one component fails, the entire circuit stops working

correct_answer: The total current is the sum of currents through each branch
explanation: In a parallel circuit, the current from the power source splits into separate branches. Each branch can have a different current, but the total current supplied by the source equals the sum of all the currents flowing through each individual branch. This is different from a series circuit where the same current flows through all components.
